mcqs.question: What is the root of a parse tree?

options.text: Start symbol
Terminal symbol
Non-terminal symbol
Production rule

mcqs.explanation: The root of a parse tree is the start symbol, which represents the beginning of the sentence.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between a parse tree and an abstract syntax tree (AST)?

options.text: A parse tree is more compact and efficient than an AST
An AST is more compact and efficient than a parse tree
A parse tree is easier to visualize and understand than an AST
An AST is easier to visualize and understand than a parse tree

mcqs.explanation: An AST is more compact and efficient than a parse tree, but less intuitive and more difficult to visualize.

mcqs.question: What is ambiguity in grammars and languages?

options.text: A grammar is ambiguous if it has multiple start symbols
A grammar is ambiguous if it has multiple production rules for the same non-terminal symbol
A grammar is ambiguous if there exists a sentence that can be parsed in more than one way
A grammar is ambiguous if it has multiple terminal symbols

mcqs.explanation: A grammar is ambiguous if there exists a sentence that can be parsed in more than one way, meaning that there are multiple possible parse trees for the same sentence.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of a parse tree?

options.text: To represent the syntactic structure of a sentence
To represent the semantic meaning of a sentence
To parse a sentence and identify its components
To generate a sentence from a set of production rules

mcqs.explanation: The purpose of a parse tree is to represent the syntactic structure of a sentence, showing how the sentence is derived from the start symbol of a grammar.

mcqs.question: How do you identify the start symbol of a parse tree?

options.text: By looking at the root of the tree
By looking at the leaves of the tree
By looking at the internal nodes of the tree
By looking at the production rules of the grammar

mcqs.explanation: The start symbol of a parse tree is the symbol at the root of the tree, which represents the beginning of the sentence.
